title: Impound Incidents
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total FROM GetImpounded WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate
detailQuery: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(Cost), 0) AS recovered FROM ClearImpound WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate

details.text: {total} ship impound incident(s) recorded during the report period. Getting impounded is expensive and operationally humiliating — it means a station's authority had enough of your criminal record and seized your vessel. Even one impound event is a memorable session story, and a high count reveals a pattern of persistent station-level antagonism.
details.help: GetImpounded fires on impound; ClearImpound when recovered. total drives the title as the impound incident count.

tagLines: Ship impound incidents logged. Station authority intervention events noted in the security record.
They took your ship. You had to buy it back. The lesson was expensive.
Impound is what happens when fines and bounties stop being a deterrent. The stations noticed.
